─[ LXC Container Gotchas (Proxmox) ]─ ┐

name: lxc-container-gotchas description: LXC tmpfs, networking, Ceph client, and permission gotchas user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob

LXC Container Gotchas (Proxmox)

Context

Common pitfalls when deploying and debugging LXC containers in Proxmox, covering runtime persistence, networking, and storage.

Snags

Runtime Directory Loss (/var/run tmpfs)

LXC mounts /var/run (and /run) as tmpfs. All contents are lost on reboot. Services that depend on directories in /var/run/ (NFS Ganesha, PostgreSQL, Nginx) will fail to start. Fix: Create /etc/tmpfiles.d/<service>.conf:

d /var/run/<service> 0755 root root -

Apply immediately: systemd-tmpfiles --create.

Docker-in-LXC Compatibility

Docker 29.x does NOT work in LXC due to a sysctl permission issue (reopen fd 8: permission denied). Workaround: Use Podman instead (with fuse-overlayfs and nesting=1 enabled in LXC).

ZFS Child Datasets

Bind-mounting a ZFS dataset into a container does NOT include its child datasets. They appear as empty directories inside the container. Workaround: Use recursive bind (rbind) instead of bind in the LXC config:

lxc.mount.entry: /source target none rbind,create=dir 0 0

Ceph Keyring Permissions

CephFS clients in containers need /etc/ceph/ceph.client.<name>.keyring with proper permissions. Gotcha: Keyring strings in configs (like Ganesha) MUST be quoted: Secret_Access_Key = "AQB...".

Prevention

  • Nesting: Always enable nesting=1 for containers running Docker/Podman or nested filesystems.
  • Unquoted Keys: Always quote Ceph keys in configuration files.
  • IP Ranges: Use non-overlapping IP ranges for hosts and containers on the same bridge to avoid ARP conflicts.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
